improvement: Implement a simple time-of-day heuristic to quickly respond with "good morning," "good afternoon," or "good evening" instead of waiting for a full user interaction to update its greeting. This would save response time on initial user interactions.

Introduce a timeout after which JARVIS returns a cached version of the app on screen description and quick actions. This way, if JARVIS is slow to process a request, it won't take as long to respond and return something useful to the user.

Based on the conversations, a small improvement could be to add contextual hints when the user is not on a specific app or screen. For example, if the user asks for actions without an app on screen, suggest checking the recent apps list or asking about the last opened app. This improvement can help reduce ambiguity and improve response accuracy.
